articles.title: TDP to introduce 19 resolutions during two-day hybrid Mahanadu from May 27
articles.description: TDP's Mahanadu on May 27-28 will introduce 19 resolutions, broadcast live to state clusters, says MLA Somireddy Chandramohan Reddy.

articles.title: Rubio offers Cubans 'new path' as US signals tougher stance on Cuba leadership
articles.description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio urged Cubans to embrace a “new path” and criticised Cuba’s leadership, as reports suggest Washington may indict former president Raúl Castro amid rising tensions.

articles.title: India can help bring peace to Europe, says Estonia's Foreign Minister
articles.description: Estonia's Foreign Minister Margus Tsahkna praised growing India-Estonia ties in tech and AI. He urged India to use its global influence to apply diplomatic pressure on Russia to help bring peace to Europe amid the ongoing Ukraine conflict.

articles.title: Delhi HC seeks response on PIL for CBI probe into fake universities
articles.description: The Delhi High Court sought responses from the Centre, UGC, and others on a PIL demanding a CBI probe into fake universities allegedly issuing invalid degrees. A bench noted the 'serious issues' raised concerning students and the education system.

articles.title: Three-language policy: Why Tamil Nadu sees an old threat in the new CBSE circular
articles.description: India's three-language policy is back at the centre of national debate, and the argument is the same one it has always been. The CBSE's 2026 mandate to make three languages compulsory up to Class 10 is framed as a step towards multilingualism and national integration. In Tamil Nadu and much of the South, it is being read as something else entirely. The question is not really about languages. It never was.

articles.title: AAP to 'break its own record' in Punjab polls, says Arvind Kejriwal
articles.description: AAP National Convenor Arvind Kejriwal expressed confidence in the party breaking its own record in Punjab's upcoming elections. Citing a 'pro-incumbency' wave, he credited the developmental work of the Bhagwant Mann-led government for the positive sentiment.

articles.title: Exclusive | Swedish Prime Minister Kristersson: India-EU Ties Not Living Up To Full Potential Yet

articles.title: Italy PM Giorgia Meloni Speaks Hindi During Joint Presser With PM Modi | Viral ‘Melodi’ Moment
articles.description: Italian PM Giorgia Meloni surprised many by speaking in Hindi during a joint press conference with PM Narendra Modi in Rome. Her remark quickly went viral online as India and Italy announced a major upgrade in bilateral ties, elevating relations to a “Special Strategic Partnership.”

articles.title: Simultaneous polls can save ₹7 lakh crore: BJP MP Chaudhary
articles.description: Joint Parliamentary panel’s recommendations propose synchronising Lok Sabha, Assembly elections in 1st phase, followed by Panchayat, Municipality polls within 100 days of general elections

articles.title: Chinese Proverb of the Day: “Women hold up half the... — Life lessons on empowerment shared responsibility, social progress and why equality benefits everyone
articles.description: Chinese Proverb of the Day highlights the meaning of “Women hold up half the sky.” The saying emphasizes the essential role women play in society, family life, leadership, and progress. It reflects the belief that the world cannot function or advance without the contributions, strength, and efforts of women. The proverb also promotes the idea of equality, suggesting that women deserve the same recognition, opportunities, and respect as men in every aspect of life.

articles.title: 'What is wrong with it?' On caste census, SC says govt should know number of people in backward class
articles.description: The Supreme Court has upheld the Centre's decision to conduct caste enumeration in the upcoming 2027 Census, stating there is "nothing wrong with it." A bench led by the Chief Justice emphasized that such data is crucial for the government to identify backward classes and implement effective welfare measures. This marks the first comprehensive caste count since 1931.

articles.title: War Will 'Spread Far Beyond Region' If US, Israel Resume Attacks: Iran Guards
articles.description: The statement comes after remarks by US President Donald Trump, who said Washington could strike Iran again if the two sides fail to reach a lasting settlement in the coming days.

articles.title: Politicising Zubeen Garg's death backfired on Congress-led opposition in Assam polls: Himanta Biswa Sarma
articles.description: Assam CM Himanta Biswa Sarma visited Zubeen Garg's cremation site, asserting the Congress-led opposition paid for politicizing the musician's death during polls. He emphasized Garg as an emotion, not a political tool, and reviewed development work at 'Zubeen Khetra' as a tribute to the beloved artist. The opposition had criticized the government over alleged delays in justice for Garg.

articles.title: IT employees feel the squeeze as firms redraw salary structures under labour-code shift
articles.description: Compensation experts, staffing firms, and labour lawyers told Moneycontrol that the changes are not isolated to TCS and reflect a broader shift underway across India’s IT and corporate sector as firms prepare for implementation of the new labour codes.

articles.title: Your Kundli May Already Know the Exact Date You’ll Get a Government Job | Ft. Pawan Sinha
articles.description: Can astrology really predict your chances of getting a government job? In this discussion, Pawan Sinha explains how kundli combinations, planetary periods, and career houses may influence success in competitive exams and stable government careers. Why do some people face repeated delays despite years of preparation? Could your birth chart reveal the right timing for career breakthroughs and exam success? This conversation explores government job yog, astrology predictions, career timing, and kundli analysis for aspirants preparing for government exams. Watch till the end for deeper insights.#timesmantra #governmentjob #kundli #astrology #govtexam #careersuccess #pawnsinha #jobprediction #horoscope #upsc #ssc #indiastrology

articles.title: Who is Mahmoud Ahmadinejad, US and Israel's pick to lead Iran?

Now, it has been revealed that they wanted Mahmoud Ahmadinejad to lead the country. Ironically, during his time in office, the former president was widely derided in the West for his anti-American and anti-Israeli stance

articles.title: China’s Xi Welcomes Putin ‘Like A King’, Takes 'Veiled Dig' At U.S. After Trump Visit | Watch
articles.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping praised China-Russia ties as a force of “calm amid chaos” during high-profile talks with Russian President Vladimir Putin in Beijing. The meeting came just days after Xi hosted President Donald Trump for a major U.S.-China summit, with analysts pointing to Xi’s remarks as a veiled swipe at Washington’s global influence. As tensions between China, Russia, and the West continue to grow, both leaders projected unity and deepening strategic coordination in the face of what Beijing called “unilateral hegemonic currents.”

articles.title: Iqra Hasan's ‘don’t touch me’ video goes viral after brief detention following clash with DIG over Shamli youth death case
articles.description: Samajwadi Party MP Iqra Hasan was briefly detained and filmed repeatedly saying "don't touch me" while being escorted by police in Saharanpur. The incident occurred after she accompanied the mother of a deceased youth to the DIG office, demanding a wider probe into his death.

articles.title: Govt pursuing policy of 'continued calibrated capitulation' to China: Cong
articles.description: The Congress on Wednesday targeted the Modi government over its approach towards Beijing and accused it of pursuing a "4C policy of continued, calibrated capitulation to China". Congress general secretary in-charge communications Jairam Ramesh also raised the issue of the Great Nicobar project to attack the government. In a post on X, Ramesh claimed, "The Modi Govt has now launched a propaganda campaign through its ecosystem to portray all those concerned about the ecological havoc that will be caused by the Great Nicobar Island Project as being 'soft on China'". "This is the height of hypocrisy, coming as it does from a government that practices the 4C policy - Continuing, Calibrated Capitulation to China," he said in his post. The Congress leader said it was the prime minister who gave an "inexplicable clean chit" to China on June 19, 2020, in a "brazen insult" to the 20 jawans martyred earlier in Ladakh. "It is the Modi Government that has, in negotiations with China, given up

articles.title: Tunisha Death Case: Family Seeks Second Postmortem, Court to Decide on Fresh Examination Today
articles.description: The family of actor Tunisha Sharma has intensified its demand for a second postmortem examination as the legal battle surrounding the high-profile death case continues to gather attention. According to sources close to the proceedings, the family has requested that the second postmortem be conducted at All India Institute of Medical Sciences, citing concerns over the initial investigation and medical examination process. The plea also reportedly seeks a transfer of the investigation from the current jurisdiction to Uttar Pradesh or, alternatively, supervision of the ongoing Special Investigation Team (SIT) by a retired Supreme Court judge. The family believes an independent and closely monitored probe is necessary to ensure transparency and public trust in the investigation. The matter has now reached a critical stage, with the court scheduled to hear arguments on the second postmortem demand. A key factor adding urgency to the proceedings is that Tunisha Sharma’s body has reportedly remained in the mortuary for nearly eight days, increasing pressure on authorities and the judiciary to arrive at a decision quickly. Legal experts suggest that the court’s verdict on whether permission for a second postmortem will be granted could significantly impact the direction of the investigation. The hearing is expected to address both procedural concerns and the family’s demand for a more independent inquiry into the circumstances surrounding the actor’s death. The case continues to attract nationwide attention, with emotional appeals from the family and intense public scrutiny over the handling of the investigation and forensic procedures.

articles.title: The Iran War Is Not Restarting: Trump Cannot Afford It
articles.description: A 75-day conflict between the US and Iran has exposed the limits of American military power, with rising costs, aircraft losses, and no clear political victory despite intense airstrikes. The piece argues that lasting peace will require compromise on Iran’s nuclear program and the Strait of Hormuz, as Washington faces the reality that military force alone cannot reshape the Middle East.

articles.title: The Modi-Meloni Chemistry: How Italy Became The Heart Of India's New Foreign Policy Bet
articles.description: The internet noticed their chemistry first. Clips of PM Modi and Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni sharing warm, unscripted moments at multilateral summits racked up millions of views. But while the world was watching the smiles, India was building an architecture. A corridor. A cable. A trade deal that could define India's next decade. Washington is unpredictable. Beijing is strategically complicated. Moscow is geopolitically diminished. In that vacuum, Europe — and Italy in particular — has emerged as India's most consequential new bet. The IMEC corridor anchors Italy as Europe's gateway to the Indo-Pacific. A fibre-optic cable now connects Indian servers to the port of Genoa. Bilateral trade has crossed $16.77 billion. Tata's $3.8 billion Iveco acquisition made history. And Modi is pushing hard for the India-EU Free Trade Agreement. The Modi-Meloni bond is not a photo opportunity. It is foreign policy in motion.
